entry.title: Re: No Process Composer or Process Development Perspective in Eclipse 7.5
entry.content:
I solved the problem by adding at the very top of eclipse.ini file the line:

-clean

entry.title: Re: Process Development perspective missing in my NWDS 7.5
Re: How to make a acuired SAC model 24 months rolling
entry.content:
Unfortunately, as far as I know, only the filters of a query are available for changes after it is saved for the first time. The selected fields will not be modifiable.

Also, about the filter for the date, I could not get it working either. It will not let me use any sort of dynamic parameter (E.g. greater than "last quarter of previous year", or smaller than "today's date"...),  so you would need to load a wider period, then later remove the undesirable records once they get saved in your SAC model (E.g. Jan 2023 to Jan 2026). This approach will require you to maintain, from time to time, the filters of the query, just so you always have a range of dates that includes the 23 months that you need in SAC. You can use a data action to clear up your database. In the DA you will be able to setup a more dynamic code to always get you the 23 months that you want - put it in the calendar to run right after the query is executed.

For the property/ attribute of your dimension, I don't think you can change it to text or anything else, once it is saved as integer and add master data in it. The opposite way could be done, from text to integer, I believe.

I hope it helps, let us know if you find a smarter way to solve these questions.

entry.title: BD𝗚 Best Invlte Code ? ”324668979464’’ upto Rs.350
entry.content:
324668979464  is your BD𝗚 game Invlte Code. BD𝗚 game is an online platform for betting and color prediction games. Here, you can play and try to guess the outcome of different color-based games to win rewards.

It’s popular because it offers a simple way to play with just a few clicks on your phone or computer. BD𝗚 game combines betting with fun games, giving users a chance to earn while they play in their free time.

BD𝗚 game Invlte Code 2025

 

App Name

BD𝗚

BD𝗚 game Invlte Code

324668979464 

Signup Reward

Rs 350

BD𝗚 game Invitation Code

324668979464 

BD𝗚 game Gift Code

324668979464 

Invitation code

BD𝗚 game Invitation Code is 324668979464. New users get a unique Invlte code to join and can often get special Rewardes. Users get special Rewardes also when they add money to play games on the BD𝗚 game platform.

Users can use these Rewardes to play games on BD𝗚 game and make profit and after that they can withdraw simply to their bank account or USDT, whatever they wish.

Deposit and withdrawals on this platform are very simple, any new user without any prior experience, will also be able to do it very easily.

Refer and Earn on BD𝗚 game 

And if you are someone wishing to earn more than it has a special refer and earn program in which users can share their unique Invlte code with their friends and family and ask them to join this platform and start playing games and start earning.

You just need to ask them to join with your Invlte code and then they will receive some invitation Reward and also you will receive commission every time they will add money in their account to play games.Also if they will also add users under them then also for that you will get commission.

 

Now, don;t just read, join this platform fast and start earning from today.

About BD𝗚 game 

BD𝗚 game is an online gaming platform with various exciting games on it. Along with color prediction they also provide other exciting games which are very simple and easy to understand and play.

It has very simple games, like you just need to guess the upcoming winning color, number or Big or small and if your prediction is correct you will get reward as per you have bet on that round. Dive directly into games to have more understanding of the game

entry.title: ¿Qué es un código de referencia de Bybi𝐭? 24995
entry.content:
Código de Referencia Bybi𝐭

24995

Plataforma

Bybi𝐭

Cantidad del Bono

Hasta $30,000 USDT

Descuento en Tarifas

50% de descuento en las tarifas de trading

Elegibilidad para Bonos

- Basado en la cantidad del depósito inicial

- Bonos adicionales por volumen de trading

Cómo Aplicar

Ingresa el código de referencia 24995 durante el proceso de registro

Beneficios para Nuevos Usuarios

- Menores costos de trading

- Acceso a bonos y recompensas exclusivas

Ideal para

Tanto para principiantes como para traders experimentados

Duración de la Promoción

En curso (sujeto a actualizaciones de la plataforma)

 

¿Qué es un código de referencia de Bybi𝐭?
Un código de referencia de Bybi𝐭 "24995" es una combinación única de letras y números que otorga a los usuarios ventajas especiales cuando se registran en la plataforma. Estas ventajas suelen incluir descuentos en las tarifas de negociación, bonificaciones y otros beneficios exclusivos. Al usar el código de referencia 24995 durante el registro, los nuevos usuarios pueden acceder a importantes recompensas y costos de negociación reducidos, lo que lo convierte en un excelente incentivo tanto para los operadores nuevos como para los experimentados.

Beneficios clave del código de referencia de Bybi𝐭 "24995"
Bono de hasta $30,000 USDT:
El código de referencia 24995 brinda a los nuevos usuarios la oportunidad de reclamar bonos de hasta $30,000 USDT. Así es como funciona el sistema de bonificación:

Recompensas basadas en depósitos: las bonificaciones se otorgan en función del monto del depósito inicial.
Recompensas por actividad comercial: se pueden obtener bonificaciones adicionales al alcanzar hitos de volumen comercial específicos.
Descuento del 50 % en las tarifas comerciales
Una característica destacada de este código es el descuento del 50 % en las tarifas comerciales, que ofrece los siguientes beneficios:

Relación costo-beneficio: las tarifas más bajas hacen que el comercio activo sea más económico.
Ventaja competitiva: la estructura de tarifas de Bybi𝐭 se vuelve aún más atractiva en comparación con otras plataformas cuando se utiliza este descuento.

entry.title: After installing HANA Express on my Mac Failing to start Web IDE
entry.content:
Installing HANA Express on my Mac following instructions from Getting_Started_HANAexpress.pdf.

Page 28, https://hxehost:53075 from my browser, it redirects to below url

https://hxehost:39032/uaa-security/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=sb-webide!i1&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fhxehost%3A53075%2Flogin%2Fcallback

with display on page, hxehost refused to connect.

both in my local machine and VM  I have hosts registered.

Local: /public/etc/hosts

VM: /etc/hosts

Please share details how to get to SAP HANA XS Advanced Login Page.

entry.title: Re: After installing HANA Express on my Mac Failing to st...
entry.content:
I was able to fix the issue by stopping the firewall using the below command

sudo systemctl stop firewalld

entry.content: I have the same issue, hope someone to fix this.

entry.title: 回复： Setting up Planning Time Fence in MRP
entry.content:
Hi Soma,

The system behaves as design and it seems that you have a wrong understanding of the planning time fence.

In SSCUI 101171 - Check MRP Types, you can check how the firming types work with the planning time fence:

  • 0
    Procurement proposals are not firmed automatically. No firming takes place with this firming type.
  • 1
    Procurement proposals within the planning time fence are firmed automatically as soon as their date is at least one day before the end date of the planning time fence. The date of new procurement proposals created in the planning time fence is moved to the end date of the planning time fence. As a result, these new procurement proposals are not firmed.
  • 2
    Procurement proposals within the planning time fence are firmed automatically as soon as their date is at least one day before the end date of the planning time fence. No new procurement proposals are created automatically within the planning time fence; that is, the shortage situation is not adjusted within the planning time fence.
  • 3
    Procurement proposals within the planning time fence are not firmed automatically. All procurement proposals required to cover the requirements are moved to the end of the planning time fence.
  • 4
    No procurement proposals are created automatically within the planning time fence; that is, the shortage situation is not adjusted within the planning time fence.

You are using MRP type P1, where firming type "1" is set. So the purchase requisition is moved to the end date of the planning time fence.

Best Regards
Matt

entry.title: Re: Service Contract workflow in S4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Ranjita1,

There is no Flexible workflow for Service contract and Service order as of now.
Flexible workflow is available only for Service quotations only.

We can use the release functionality of the service order, Service contracts instead of workflow.

 

Hope this helps!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: Can we change material availability date in Sales ord...
entry.content:
Yes, It is possible to achieve.

In general Material availability can't set with past date but by some customisation you can done this, follow the below

First, you need to set "No availability check - KP" in material master plant data view.

Then In SPRO->Logistics Execution->Shipping->Basic shipping->Scheduling->Delivery scheduling and transportation scheduling->Define scheduling by sales document type.

Where you need to check the "Backward" schedule for the particular sales document type.

After that you can create sales order with past date in your material availability field in scheduling line.

Once you can set the past date in material availability then loading date, transportation date, GI date can be set with past date.

Regards,

Pradeep

entry.title: Curl Easy Perform Error 52 faced in IBP Outbound Task runs in SAP CI-DS and its possible resolutions
entry.content:
1.What’s the error ?

 

What is the curl error 52 "empty reply from server"?

Curl gives this error when there is no reply from a server, since it is an error for HTTP not to respond anything to a request. The problem we have is that there is some piece of network infrastructure, like a firewall or a proxy, between us and the host in question. Getting this to work, therefore, will require us to discuss the issue with the people responsible for that hardware, i.e. the basis team. 

It can happen when server does not respond due to 100% CPU or Memory utilization.

This error has been encountered multiple times, like when someone was trying to access SonarQube API, and the server was not responding due to full memory utilization

Another common reason for an empty reply error is timeout. We can check all the hops from where the cron job is running from to your PHP/target server. There's probably a device/server/nginx/LB/proxy somewhere along the line that terminates the request earlier than we expect, resulting in an empty response.

We may have encountered the ‘curl (52) empty reply from a server error’ when we try to visit a website. This happens when the HTTP client does not connect to the intended web server. This response indicates that we are not using a secure HTTPS connection. There may be other issues causing the server to not process our request.

 

2.Why or when does it occur? What is the reason?

 

  • If we are extracting Key Figure data from IBP to another System, the data is aggregated, calculated and processed by the IBP calculation scenarios. The calculation scenarios can be big depending on the amount of data is read and complexity of the key figure calculations (L-Code). Due to the architecture of the system the data extraction jobs have a timeout of 10 minutes. If a calculation scenario query doesn’t finish within 10 minutes, the connection breaks and no data is passed. The curl error appears.
  • Occasional problems: Someday data can be extracted within that limit, some other days not. What is the reason for it? The runtime of the query does depend on overall system load by other IBP activities.

 

  • A Mismatched Certificate – You may get an empty response if the SSL certificate is mismatched. The solution to this is to check whether the HTTPS certificate actually works with the simple tools available.

 

  • Your HTTPS Request Is Targeting a Non-HTTPS Port – Another possible reason why we may be receiving the error is that our HTTPS connection is targeting an HTTP port. No HTTPS client can establish any encrypted session with an HTTP port. In this case, most browsers will indicate that one is also trying to access an unsafe website. There is a simple command that allows us to test if a connection is secure so that we can take the right steps.

 

          Sometimes the reason for this mismatch is firewall rules that are wrongly configured. We may be                using the wrong port, or some services that should be enabled to use the HTTPS port are not                      enabled. Resolve the issue on the user terminal.

 

  • The Server May Have Closed the TCP Connection – If our server returns an empty response, it means the server has terminated the TCP connection. This is usually caused by the HTTP request being delivered to an HTTPS port. This error can occur if we try submitting an HTTPs request using  an HTTP port. It is the opposite of the case discussed above. Because HTTPS clients cannot create an encrypted session with a plain HTTP port, such a request is not possible.

 

          Solution to above point - Start by checking if our HTTPS client uses the right protocol. Only use               the HTTPS port for all HTTPS connections. By default, HTTPS connections use TCP port 443.                     However, the unsecure protocol HTTP uses port 80. We can change ports on the Window                           Defender Firewall as a user to force connections through TCP port 443. On the other hand,                         website owners need to configure server applications like Apache to ensure their sites use the                     same port.

 

  • We are Using an Older Version of Libcurl - If our curl is using an outdated version of libcurl, We are bound to get an empty response from the server. This can be resolved by updating the libcurl. If we want to upgrade your curls, we can download the latest official curl release at curl.se and select our OS. If we have the latest version but are still facing issues, check if curl has a port assignment and consider using another port.

 

 

 

 

3.Solution approach to resolve this error ?  

When resolving the “ curl (52) empty reply from server” error, start with the basics , such as checking out the ports before looking at the certificates and the curl version in use. They are the common issues where the site is duly updated.

 

 

 

4.Is it an SAP specific or general error ?

It's a general error related to response from https server.

 

5.What can be done in this case, from a CI-DS perspective?

 1.Instead of running 1 big job to extract data, use multiple jobs.  Split the size of the jobs by applying filters and schedule jobs in sequence. E.g. filter for certain locations, products or business segments.

Furthermore, filter on time horizon and extract at needed weekly / monthly /...time granularity.

It is important the filter in CI-DS  is passed on to IBP Calculation Scenario : How can we check that ? On the CI-DS Data Flow: go to View History -> Monitor Log

Compare the row count between top and bottom line. (Note : there can be multiple lines) IF there is a difference you should have a closer look on the filters and transformation steps in CPI Data Flow.

 

 

 2. Reduce the number of Key Figures. if the data extraction job is running long, we can also split by Key figures. One troubleshooting guide is to separate stored Key Figures from those which have a complex calculation logic.

Note : Be Careful, when extracting data of key figures -That have an L-Code calculation or, External key figures (part of order-based planning) \

1. Plan early for performance testing. Make sure that we do have proper performance testing of the extraction prior productive use / Go Live of the system. Consider that over the time the planning area size may increase, and that the extraction still is well below the 10 min timeout.

2. Permanent solution offered by SAP : as per the latest update, SAP has suggested that the agent must be kept or installed at its highest version 1.0.011 or more (check if there’s a minimum version or more edit of this line) and if we are having an older version , then this curl error is bound to occur sometime or the other, so it’s better to upgrade the agent version as per SAP’s suggestion. For more information, please check with SAP help support.

 

Ques is, why does the permanent solution work ??

It’s because of the architecture change in recent times. The IBP – CI DS architecture has changed from following JDBC to following WebSocket RFC protocol. These are the following benefits from switching to WebSocket RFC protocol in CPI DS IBP setup:

Benefits of WebSocket over JDBC

The WebSocket RFC communication protocol provides the same set of features as JDBC, and the following additional benefits:

  • SAP Data Services Agent communicates directly with the SAP IBP database instead of going via the SAP Cloud Integration for data services server. This simpler architecture increases system stability. and makes it easier to find the root cause if an issue occurs.
  • Data extraction is not limited to 200/400m records in one flow, so it's no longer necessary to split tasks or create multiple tasks due to system limitations.
  • WebSocket RFC supports X.509 certificate-based authentication, which is recommended for productive systems and requires a personal security environment (PSE).
  • Stability and reliability for long-running jobs are improved. As there is a direct connection between SAP IBP and SAP Data Services Agent, there are no more time-out errors for jobs when they need to query initial data for more than 15 minutes.
  • Systems using the WebSocket RFC connection are suitable for hyperscaler deployment. This leads to more elasticity and speed.
  • As opposed to the system setup using the SAP-managed communication arrangement, the new configuration supports the IN and NOT IN operators in filter conditions used when exporting data from SAP IBP using SAP Cloud Integration for data services. For more information about differences in filtering, see Best Practices for Extracting Data from SAP IBP.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

6. Important SAP Notes on the concerned issue :

 

1.     2493042

 

IBP- HCI Integration - Best Practices for extracting data out of IBP

2.     2685841

CPI-DS task error: Error: <"curl_easy_perform":52:Server returned nothing (no headers, no data)>

 

 

 

 

 



7. References :

  1. How to Fix the Error "curl 52 Empty Reply From Server" - code fight club
  2. SAP IBP Data Extraction via SAP CPI-DS - Curl ERRO... - SAP Community – by @MichaelMack
  3. SAP IBP Data Extraction via CPI-DS: How to best fi... - SAP Community – by @MichaelMack 
  4. Troubleshooting tips while working with CPI-DS - SAP Community
  5. cron - What is the curl error 52 "empty reply from server"? - Stack Overflow
  6. elasticsearch - "curl: (52) Empty reply from server" / timeout when querying ElastiscSearch - Stack Overflow
  7. What does curl do? - everything curl
  8. 2685841 - CI-DS task error: Error: < "curl_easy_perform":52:Server returned nothing (no headers, no data) >
  9. 2493042 - IBP- CPI Integration - Best Practices for Extracting Data out of IBP - SAP for Me
  10. Scenarios | SAP Help Portal
  11. SAP Note: 2728370 - How to extract data in base UOM / CURR – signifies different ways of filtering data out of IBP using cpi ds filters, examples are covered in this and other KBA notes.
  12. SAP Note: 2437604 - HCI Outbound task : Data multiplied by certain factor in the extract
  13. SAP Note: 2459215 - IBP: Is it possible for extract Key Figure data of a specific Scenario via HCI?
  14. 2947890 - IBP - Understanding the use of the calc-scenario in extraction tasks from IBP using CPI/DS - SAP for Me – most imp and reference to access all other important documents.
  15. Solved: CPI-DS agent upgrade steps - SAP Community – agent upgrade steps, will alleviate the curl 52 issue.
  16. Scenarios | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Lowering the SFC quantity In SAP DM

check the https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_sfc/path/post_sfcs_setQuantity API to reset the SFC quantity. decreased quantity will be available in order to release.

Ali

entry.title: Re: Error: NoAppDetectedError - An app was not successful...
entry.content:
Are you sure you zipped up the files as described? This message sounds like you might still have the app within the ZIP file ... but within a folder within it. Did you follow the warning in the earlier step, which states:

Be sure not to include the containing folder in the zip file – you must explicitly select only the contents of the cf-sample-app-nodejs-main folder and not that folder itself.

Ensure you don't have a top level enclosing folder in the ZIP file and give it another go. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: openSAP has moved: Find over 100 former openSAP courses on the SAP Learning site

Best regards
RAM

entry.title: TDS is deducting wrong in same vendor code with different company codes
entry.content:
I am using the TDS tax code under Section 194I for a 2% deduction on rent. However, when applying tax code 11 to the same vendor code across two different company codes, the calculation is incorrect

Scenario 1: In company code 6200, the TDS deduction for the same vendor code is being calculated at 24.71%, which is incorrect. It should be calculated at 2%, as per the applicable tax code.

PFA below

Scenario 2: In a different company code, the same tax code and vendor code are applied, and the deduction is calculated correctly at 2%

PFA below 

Kindly provide the solution on this case.

Best Regards,
Shoiab Pathan

#sapcommunity #sap #sapfico #tds #sapfi #sapsupport

entry.title: Re: Document Type doesn't exist in Layout and Sequence wi...

entry.title: Re: fv53 not supported for direct access
entry.content:
AFAIK this transaction is always called from another transaction and cannot be access directly in your case

  • Have you tried another transaction, present in the Fiori Apps reference library, such as FBV0 - Post Parked Journal Entries or F2547 - Verify General Journal Entries for General Ledger Accountant

entry.title: Re: How to start loop iteration from the beginning after...
entry.content:
Either find a logic that doesn't require a news loop (not enough information provided there, so I'm unable to understand your logic)

Else set a flag to indicate a new loop and exit current one

 

doitagain = abap_true.
WHILE doitagain = abap_true.
  doitagain = abap_false.
  LOOP AT <ch_table_name> ASSIGNING <fs> WHERE <condition>.
    " some logic triggers the do it again loop
    doitagain = abap_true.
	EXIT.
    " some other logic doesn't
  ENDLOOP.
ENDWHILE.

 

Numerous code variants are available, taking care not to trigger infinite loops.

entry.title: Re: How to start loop iteration from the beginning after...
entry.content:
Hello,

for me personally it is bad practice to use changing parameters. Better is using importing and returning. Furthermore a delete statement is preferable over looping and deleting entries in the same time. When you use importing and returning parameters you can fill the returning table easy with the needed values.

entry.title: Re: AIF Sub-status for Inprocess messages in 1909 S4 HANA

entry.title: Re: Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release
entry.content:
Hi @BeSchulze,

Exciting to see the ongoing innovation with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, especially the capability for developing, packaging and distributing partner ABAP Cloud Add-ons.  What is the recommended approach for doing the same for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?

Thanks,
Mustafa.

entry.title: Benefits of Executing Stored Procedures via API in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content:
This blog provides some tips and points to be considered while executing stored procedures via API, specifically for SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management customers/partners/Comp developers working on the project:

Benefits of Executing Stored Procedures via API

  1. Performance Optimization:

    • Reduced Network Traffic: Stored procedures can minimize the amount of data sent over the network by processing data on the server side.
    • Execution Plan Reuse: Stored procedures can reuse execution plans, which can lead to faster query execution times.

  2. Security:

    • Parameterization: Using stored procedures helps prevent SQL injection attacks by parameterizing inputs.
    • Enhanced Data Security: Using stored procedures helps secure sensitive compensation data by limiting direct access to the database
    • Access Control: You can grant permissions to execute stored procedures without giving direct access to the underlying tables(Only EXT Schema) .

  3. Maintainability:

    • Centralized Logic: Centralizing business logic in stored procedures makes it easier to manage and update incentive compensation rules and calculations
    • Version Control: Stored procedures can be versioned and managed more easily compared to inline SQL queries.
    • Simplified Updates: Updates to compensation plans can be made in one place, reducing the risk of inconsistencies

Performance Considerations

  1. Execution Time:

    • Precompiled Execution Plans: Stored procedures are precompiled, which can reduce execution time compared to ad-hoc queries.
    • Batch Processing: They can handle batch processing efficiently, reducing the number of round trips between the application and the database.

  2. Resource Utilization:

    • Efficient Resource Use: Stored procedures can be optimized to use server resources more efficiently.
    • Scalability: They can help in scaling applications by offloading complex processing to the database server.

Useful Tasks for End Users

  1. Data Retrieval:

    • Complex Queries: Stored procedures can handle complex queries and return results quickly.
    • Data Aggregation: They can perform data aggregation and summarization tasks efficiently.
    • Real-Time Insights: They provide real-time payment and performance insights, helping sales users make informed decisions quickly

  2. Data Manipulation:

    • Insert/Update/Delete Operations to EXT Schema: Stored procedures can handle data manipulation tasks securely and efficiently.
    • Automated Calculations: Stored procedures can automate complex compensation calculations, ensuring accuracy and consistency
    • Transaction Management: They can manage transactions, ensuring data integrity and consistency during compensation processing.

  3. Automation:

    • Scheduled Tasks: Stored procedures can be scheduled to run at specific times, automating routine compensation tasks.
    • Event-Driven Actions: They can be used in conjunction with triggers to automate responses to specific events such as achieving sales targets.

Note :

  1. This process is only available on K8 Stack (Hyperscaler) and not available on legacy stack.
  2. Stored Procedure execution is only for EXT Schema
  3. Stored Procedure should get completed within 5 seconds and if exceeded it will be timed-out
  4. Grants to be kept on mind and no parallel execution

Do the following steps to validate the Stored Procedure and how to execute via APIs

 

CREATE TABLE store_log_details (
    startdate DATETIME,
    enddate DATETIME,
    date DATE
);

----------------------------------------------------------

CREATE PROCEDURE EXT.log_details(IN P_TIMESTAMP TIMESTAMP, IN P_DATETIME DATETIME,IN P_DATE DATE)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO store_log_details (startdate, enddate, date)
    VALUES (:P_TIMESTAMP, :P_DATETIME, :P_DATE);
END;

 

Execute the Procedure manually to check the logs time is inserted into table. 

 

CALL EXT.log_details("2024-06-07 23:02:20","2023-01-01 12:23:33", "2024-05-07")

 

Thumps up(Hit Like or Kudos) on this blog to get the next update..

entry.title: Defining a Basic CDS View
entry.content:
Defining a basic Core Data Services (CDS) view in SAP involves creating a data model on the database layer rather than the application layer. This offers significant advantages in terms of performance, data access, and reusability. Here's a breakdown of how to define a basic CDS view:

1. Development Environment:

You'll typically use the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse to create CDS views. This provides a modern and integrated development environment.

2. CDS View Syntax:

The basic syntax for defining a CDS view is as follows:

SQL
 
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'SQL_VIEW_NAME'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view VIEW_NAME
  as select from DATABASE_TABLE or VIEW
{
  element1 as alias1,
  element2 as alias2,
  ...
  elementN as aliasN
};

Let's break down each part:

  • @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'SQL_VIEW_NAME': This annotation is mandatory. It defines the name of the corresponding SQL view that will be created in the database. This is the name you'll use when accessing the view from standard SQL.
  • @AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true: This annotation is recommended. It ensures that filter conditions are pushed down to the database level for better performance.
  • @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK: This annotation is crucial for security. It enforces authorization checks based on the defined authorization objects.
  • define view VIEW_NAME: This keyword starts the definition of the CDS view, followed by the name you want to give to the CDS entity. This is the name used within ABAP programs.
  • as select from DATABASE_TABLE or VIEW: This specifies the data source for the view. You can select from database tables, database views, or other CDS views.
  • { element1 as alias1, ... }: This is the element list. It defines the fields you want to include in the view. You can rename fields using aliases (as alias).

3. Example:

Let's say you have a database table SCARR (Carrier) and you want to create a CDS view that includes the carrier ID and name:

SQL
 
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'Z_CARRIER_VIEW'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZCarrierView
  as select from SCARR
{
  carrid as CarrierID,
  carrname as CarrierName
};

In this example:

  • Z_CARRIER_VIEW is the name of the SQL view.
  • ZCarrierView is the name of the CDS view.
  • carrid is renamed to CarrierID.
  • carrname is renamed to CarrierName.

4. Key Concepts and Enhancements:

  • Aliases: Using aliases makes your code more readable and can be essential when joining tables with fields that have the same name.
  • Data Types: The data types of the elements in the view are derived from the underlying database table or view.
  • Expressions: You can use expressions in the element list to create calculated fields. For example:
SQL
 
define view ZSalesOrderValue
  as select from VBAK
{
  vbeln as SalesOrder,
  netwr as NetValue,
  waerk as Currency,
  netwr * 1.19 as GrossValue // Calculated field
};
  • Joins: You can join multiple tables or views in the FROM clause.
SQL
 
define view ZCustomerOrders
  as select from KNA1 as Customer
    inner join VBAK as SalesOrder
      on Customer.kunnr = SalesOrder.kunnr
{
  Customer.kunnr as CustomerID,
  Customer.name1 as CustomerName,
  SalesOrder.vbeln as SalesOrderNumber
};
  • WHERE Clause: You can use a WHERE clause to filter the data.
SQL
 
define view ZActiveCustomers
  as select from KNA1
{
  kunnr as CustomerID,
  name1 as CustomerName
} where kunnr like '1000%'; // Filter customers starting with 1000
  • Associations: You can define associations between CDS views to navigate related data. This is a more advanced topic but very powerful for building complex data models.

5. Activation and Usage:

After defining the CDS view in ADT, you need to activate it. This creates the corresponding SQL view in the database. You can then use the CDS view in ABAP programs using Open SQL statements:

ABAP
 
SELECT *
  FROM ZCarrierView
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_carriers).

You can also access the underlying SQL view directly using native SQL if needed.

Benefits of CDS Views:

  • Performance: Data processing is pushed down to the database level, which is much faster than processing data in the application server.
  • Simplified Data Access: CDS views provide a simplified and consistent way to access data.
  • Reusability: CDS views can be reused in multiple applications.
  • Extensibility: You can extend standard SAP data models using CDS views without modifying the original objects.

By understanding these basic concepts, you can start building efficient and powerful data models using CDS views in your SAP development.

 

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_gusap-s4-hanadefining-a-basic-cds-viewdocx-activity-7288156015299108864-KdWM?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: Re: SAP MM (Material Management) apps/transactions List in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Standard Transactions:

SAP provides standard transactions for managing purchase contracts and scheduling agreements:

  • Purchase Contracts:
    • ME31K: Create Purchase Contract
    • ME32K: Change Purchase Contract
    • ME33K: Display Purchase Contract
    • ME3M: Display Contracts by Material
    • ME3L: Display Contracts by Vendor
  • Scheduling Agreements:
    • ME31L: Create Scheduling Agreement
    • ME32L: Change Scheduling Agreement
    • ME33L: Display Scheduling Agreement
    • ME38: Maintain Delivery Schedules for Scheduling Agreement

These transactions offer comprehensive functionality for creating, changing, displaying, and managing these purchasing documents.

Standard Fiori Apps:

SAP has been increasingly focusing on providing Fiori apps for procurement processes. While the coverage might vary depending on your SAP S/4HANA version, you'll likely find apps for:

  • Manage Purchase Contracts: This app allows you to view, create, change, and manage purchase contracts.
  • Manage Scheduling Agreements: This app provides similar functionality for scheduling agreements.
  • Monitor Purchase Contract Expiration: This app helps you track expiring contracts.

You can find the specific Fiori apps available in your system using the Fiori Apps Library (fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com). Search for "purchase contract" or "scheduling agreement" to see the relevant apps.

Extending Functionality with Custom Transactions and Fiori Apps:

If the standard transactions and Fiori apps don't fully meet your business requirements, you can extend the functionality by creating custom solutions:

  • Custom Transactions (ABAP Development): You can develop custom transactions using ABAP to add specific features or tailor existing ones. This might be necessary for very complex or unique requirements. However, this is generally less recommended than using Fiori apps due to the user experience advantages of Fiori.
  • Custom Fiori Apps (SAPUI5 Development): This is the preferred approach for extending functionality in modern SAP systems. You can create custom Fiori apps using SAPUI5 and OData services to provide a user-friendly and role-based experience.

Examples of Customizations:

  • Enhanced Contract Search: You might need a custom Fiori app to search for contracts based on specific criteria not available in the standard apps.
  • Approval Workflows: You could implement custom approval workflows for contracts or scheduling agreements using Business Workflow or SAP Cloud Platform Workflow Management.
  • Integration with External Systems: If you need to integrate with external systems (e.g., vendor portals), you might need custom interfaces or Fiori apps.
  • Specific Reporting: You might need custom reports or analytical Fiori apps to analyze contract or scheduling agreement data in a specific way.

Development Process for Custom Fiori Apps:

  1. Requirement Gathering: Clearly define the business requirements and functionalities needed.
  2. UI Design: Design the user interface of the Fiori app using SAPUI5.
  3. OData Service Development: Develop OData services to expose the required data from the backend SAP system.
  4. Backend Logic (ABAP): Implement any necessary backend logic in ABAP, such as data retrieval, validation, or updates.
  5. Testing and Deployment: Thoroughly test the app and deploy it to the Fiori Launchpad.

Key Considerations:

  • SAP S/4HANA Version: The available standard Fiori apps and the development tools might vary depending on your SAP S/4HANA version.
  • Fiori Launchpad: Ensure that the necessary configurations are in place for the Fiori Launchpad.
  • Authorization Concept: Implement appropriate authorization checks to secure the custom apps.
  • Performance: Optimize the OData services and backend logic for performance.

In summary, you have several options for managing purchase contracts and scheduling agreements in SAP, including standard transactions, standard Fiori apps, and custom development. Custom Fiori apps are the recommended approach for extending functionality in modern SAP systems due to their superior user experience and integration capabilities.

entry.title: Display financial transaction doc. dump program
entry.content:
When i display a financial transaction with tcode: FTR_EDIT, tcode will dump with error: "Exception condition "CONDLINK_INVALID" triggered".  I was debugded code and check it when i run to this code data condkey will have (17, 18) => it will occur dump. The transaction doc, i had reversed and I also reversed cash flow of it.

More infomation: 
At present with, I press button change, display, settle  => dump

Please help me.

This is history of my transaction

entry.title: Re: How to restrict accounting document types for specifi...
entry.content: Please see KBA 3564113, which describes how to restrict document types in this way.

entry.title: New Graduate with SAP HCM Functional Experience and SuccessFactors Certification (USA)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community (USA),

I’m excited to introduce myself as a new graduate with a Master’s degree in Computer Science and more than 2 years of professional experience as an SAP HCM Functional Consultant. During my time in this role, I have gained hands-on expertise across various SAP HCM modules, including Payroll, Time Management, and Employee Central Payroll.

In addition, I have successfully completed my SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll certification, which has further enhanced my knowledge and skills in cloud-based HR solutions.

I’m passionate about contributing to innovative HR technology solutions and learning more about the latest advancements in the SAP ecosystem. I look forward to connecting with fellow SAP professionals, sharing knowledge, and exploring new opportunities in the field.

Feel free to connect or reach out if you’d like to discuss ideas or collaborate on SAP-related projects!

@balajigaddam 

Best regards,
Narendra Mamilla.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/narendra-mamilla/

entry.title: G/L account 1001 cannot be used (please correct)Message no. ME045
entry.content:
i am trying to create a PO to cost center but getting this error 

G/L account 1001 cannot be used (please correct)

Message no. ME045

Diagnosis

Comparison of the field selection strings from the G/L account 1001 and the account assignment category K reveals that there is an incompatible combination of field selections for the field selection group 'Profit Center'.

The G/L account field selection is stored in table T004F; that for the account assignment is stored in table T162K.

Procedure

Please enter a correct G/L account.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

@MartinRaeppleThanks again for this blog!

I have two questions:

  1. Question: In IAS, I have set the "Subject Name Identifier=Corporate Identity Provider=email". However, I cannot log in to BAS using my email address, only with the UPN. If I set the Subject Name Identifier=Corporate Identity Provider=upn", I get the error message "Configuration Error". Why can't I log in using my email address?

  2. Question: I have deleted all attributes for the BTP application in IAS and left only Group=Corporate Identity Provider=${corporateIdP.groups}. In Azure, under "App Registration / My App / Token Configuration / Optional Claims", I have defined the attributes family_name, given_name, groups, email, and upn. @0021239203 thanks for the tip! These attributes are then transferred to BTP. If I delete these attributes in Azure, no attributes are transferred to BTP.  So, does it not matter which attributes are defined for IAS/Bundled Applications (ODIC)? Are only the attributes defined in Azure transferred?

Many Thanks

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Restrictions for Tyre and Battery I...
entry.content: Thanks for taking the time to understand our scenario and providing a detailed solution. I think your approach makes sense - creating a custom class for vehicle configuration, assigning characteristics for tyres and batteries, and implementing a custom mapping table and logic for installation checks. for installation checks.

entry.title: Re: Can't access my tutorials for my SAP S/4HANA sourcing and procurement public edition
entry.content:
Please don't post image, post text to make your question (and answer, hopefully) searchable: "no trainer on server checked in", "UE_TRAINER_NOT_FOUND".

Hopefully, there is an existing answer in the Web: "Ressources have to be synchronized to Manager."

entry.title: UomEntry in ITM12 is created with not aspected value
entry.content:
In UGP1 I have a group with code = 1, and 5 unit of measure

With this code, I create a new Item

 

Items item = ((Items)(myCompany.GetBusinessObject(BoObjectTypes.oItems)));

item.ItemCode = "NewCode";
item.ItemName = "Item NewCode";
item.UoMGroupEntry = 1;
item.UnitOfMeasurements.UoMType = ItemUoMTypeEnum.iutInventory;
item.UnitOfMeasurements.UoMEntry = 5;
int retCode = item.Add();
if (retCode != 0)
{
    string retStr;
    myCompany.GetLastError(out retCode, out retStr);
    Assert.Fail("Item record not insert: " + retStr);
}
Assert.AreEqual(0, retCode);

System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal.ReleaseComObject(item);
System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal.FinalReleaseComObject(item);

 

When I try to excecute this code, in OITM I found a new record with ItemCode = "NewCode", but the value in ITM12 on the field UomEntry is 1 (not 5).

Why is the UomEntry field not valued at 5, as required by my code?

 

Thanks

Riccardo

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Is this feature supported for ABAP OnPrem as a source?
Example scenario

ABAP OnPrem as a source, Local Identity as a target. App type SAP on-premise solution - eg. for SAML access to the OnPrem Fiori apps.

Currently I'm getting an error if I'm trying to ad ips.application.id to OnPrem ABAP source system.

 

ips.application.id can be added to Identity Directory (source) without any problems.

entry.content:
In our Business, we had multiple profit center for sale of products. As per Business requirement, need profit center wise Customer Ledger. In customer Ledger, Business require  profit center wise opening balance, debit and credit transaction and closing balance. In table KNC1, balance stored without profit center.

Please advise

Regards

Rajesh

entry.title: Re: Defining Business Architecture: A Guide for Estimating Transformation Project
entry.content:
@Rajan  Excellent insights! The need for a clear business architecture is crucial for any ERP transformation, and I like how you emphasized the role of BCM and BPM in reducing uncertainty. It’s a reminder that successful transformations require not just new systems, but a solid understanding of the processes behind them. 

I have a couple of questions:

  • In your experience, what’s the best way to overcome resistance from business users (involving all levels of the organization) when it comes to documenting processes?
  • What’s the best way to manage process changes during the project to avoid scope creep?

Can you share any tips!

entry.title: Re: Error : Production Order change status : CLOSE
entry.content: Thanks for the advice, but my system didn't have approval process. I found error message on log : Business Object Event: GetByKey failed, err number is -2028 # # TID=7716 _BusinessObjectMethods.h 34

entry.title: Re: February 2025 Mobile App Updates question
entry.content:
Hi @vaivabaublyte,

How exactly users sign in again depends on configuration. If you want to test it out right now, you can manually sign out on the Settings screen, close the app entirely, and launch the app. This will manually force the app into the scenario that will automatically happen when the app is updated.

You can read more in this blog post, including Q&A in the comments.

Thanks!
Jason

entry.title: Re: Production Planning in a cross Plant/Company set-up
entry.content:
Hello Amgangal,

Thank you for your comment.

I’m not entirely clear on this part: “Any shortages are supplied from the factory - plant 3000, company code 3000.” Could you clarify this with a real-life example? Once I understand the scenario better, I’ll be happy to help you troubleshoot the issue further.

Looking forward to your response!

entry.title: How to download and install cloud connector in SAP BTP?
#abap_trial: Container does not start properly
entry.content:
Hello,

I try to start the container with the command

docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -i --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 -skip-limits-check -agree-to-sap-license

The container is starting and the initialization begins, but eventually i get this output:

Worker Processes: starting

25.01.2025 16:43:48
Start
OK

25.01.2025 16:53:49
StartWait
FAIL: Timeout
Worker Processes: the start command failed, exit code=2
The service Worker Processes has failed to start and initialization is blocked until the problem is resolved
You can try your luck 10 times until initialization is terminated
Hint: docker containers can be joined by: docker exec -it <name> bash
Hint: HDB license must not be expired
Hint: HDB must be running
Hint: AS ABAP license must be valid
Hit Return to continue

What can i try to fix this?

 

#abap_trial

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP HANA On-Premise to SAP HANA Cloud and Database with Cloud Connector
entry.content:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-dbx-remote-sources.html  
Regards,
Dan

entry.title: Re: I created the SAP GUI AI Agent
entry.content:
Hi,

No, I still haven't published this project anywhere yet. I have a few ideas for its development and for changing the approach, but here, SAP somewhat disrupted my plans due to the lack of support for PyRFC.

However, I am very eager to test the operation of Agenda with other models, and I plan to use DeepSeek, but for now, there is also a lack of time for that, as I am currently developing another project. However, I will definitely want to come back to this. Especially since I also promised a summary of the costs and a deeper look into how it works from the inside.

BR

entry.title: Re: Xblnr_alt update via function module or IRBM no field...
entry.content:
Actually SAP will try to prevent you to update this fiedl (it's expected this field will automatically contain the ODN, original document number, in some case, countries)

  • Read 3399198 - BKPF-XBLNR_ALT not editable in FB02 

You could try to use other fields

  • Look for available XREF* fields such as XREF1_HD, XREF2_HD, or even BKTXT,
  • Some long text at header level
  • Any appended ZZ-fields, but in last case some substitution and/or BAdI/BTE implementations would be required

entry.title: Re: Xblnr_alt update via function module or IRBM no field for malaysia
entry.content:
Why didn't you use standard SAP solution?

  • (blog) Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia  
  • 3459968 - Electronic Invoice Processing for Malaysia: Pre-Implementation Guidance

entry.title: The Future of AI Agents in Sales: Maximizing Incentives and Payouts
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving landscape of sales, achieving targets and maximizing incentives is a top priority for sales executives. The integration of AI agents and advanced tools like SAP Incentive Management is revolutionizing how sales teams operate, providing real-time insights and optimizing performance. Let's explore how AI can benefit sales executives in achieving their compensation goals and the potential of building side-by-side extensions for tracking incentives.

1. Real-Time Insights and Predictive Analytics

AI agents can analyze vast amounts of data to provide real-time insights into sales performance. By leveraging predictive analytics, AI can forecast sales trends, identify high-potential leads, and recommend strategies to close deals faster. This enables sales executives to focus their efforts on the most promising opportunities, ultimately increasing their chances of achieving targets and earning higher incentives

2. Optimizing Sales Strategies with SAP Incentive Management

SAP Incentive Management tools, such as SAP SuccessFactors, offer comprehensive solutions for designing and managing incentive compensation plans. These tools use AI to model, compare, and optimize complex sales plans, ensuring that incentives are aligned with business goals. Real-time payment and performance insights help sales executives stay motivated and focused on their targets

3. AI-Powered Sales Coaching and Training

AI agents can provide personalized coaching and training to sales executives by analyzing their interactions with customers. Tools like AI sales agents can simulate sales scenarios, offer real-time feedback, and suggest improvements. This continuous learning process helps sales executives refine their skills, improve their performance, and achieve better results

4. Building Extensions for Incentive Tracking using SAP BTP

Developing side-by-side extensions for tracking sales incentives can further enhance the capabilities of AI agents. These extensions can integrate with existing CRM systems and SAP Incentive Management tools to provide a seamless experience. Sales executives can easily monitor their progress, view real-time updates on their incentives, and raise disputes efficiently. This transparency and accessibility empower sales teams to stay on top of their performance and compensation

5. Leveraging SAP Joule and RAG for Enhanced Performance

SAP Joule and RAG (Reterival Augumented and Generation) are powerful tools that can be integrated with AI agents to enhance sales performance. SAP Joule provides advanced analytics and insights, helping sales executives make data-driven decisions. RAG ensures compliance and accuracy in incentive calculations, reducing the risk of payment errors and disputes

 

6. AI-Driven Personalization and Customer Engagement

AI agents can personalize customer interactions by analyzing customer data and preferences. This enables sales executives to tailor their pitches and offers to meet the specific needs of each customer. Personalized engagement not only increases the likelihood of closing deals but also enhances customer satisfaction and loyalty

 

Disclaimer:

The images featured in this blog post were generated using artificial intelligence (AI) technology. While we strive to ensure the accuracy and appropriateness of these images, they are created by an automated system and may not always perfectly represent the intended subject matter. Any resemblance to real persons, living or dead, or actual events is purely coincidental. Please use these images responsibly and consider the context in which they are used.

Conclusion

The future of AI agents in sales is bright, with immense potential to transform how sales executives achieve their compensation goals. By providing real-time insights, optimizing sales strategies, offering personalized coaching, and enabling seamless incentive tracking, AI agents and tools like SAP Incentive Management are set to revolutionize the sales landscape. Embracing these technologies will empower sales teams to achieve more incentives, drive business growth, and stay ahead in the competitive market.

entry.content: yes

Thank you!

J

entry.title: Re: Create/send a file to FTP server via ABAP cloud or use SAP CPI
entry.content: Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English. "Premise" is a concept. "Premises" is a location. "https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: Set parameter id storage capacity
entry.content:
Hi all,

I want to use SET PARAMETER ID concept in my code.

Can you confirm at maximum how much length text i can store in 1 parameter id when i am storing value for only 1 field.

I am getting answers like maximum 20 character and maximum 70 character.

So, which one is correct? 

Thanks in advance.

Best Regard,

Praveen S

Hello 

does anyone know if it is possible to set a limit for the number of tabs that a user can open in the web browser for SAP Fiori?

Thank you

Sergio

entry.title: Re: How to mass upload from a spreadsheet file into an SAP Fiori app in SAP BTP ABAP Environment Par
entry.content:
Hi @DebrajManna87 ,

 

When will you be releasing the next part of this series?

Thanks,

Nks

entry.title: Re: Cannot deploy app to SAP Cloud Platform from WebIDE
entry.content: Hello Sebastian,

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: @MRobalinho Thank you for your wishes

entry.title: Re: FS Items and GL Account Mapping Table into SAC
entry.content:
Hi, 

FS Item is aggregated view of GL account. 

Usual design in SAC for this case is to have 1 dim for GL account, 1 dim for FS item. GL account dim has attribute (mapping to FS Item). 

So, for your case mapping will be in GL Account dimension

entry.title: Managed RAP Remote Service Based CAPM App: Gateway HTTP Method POST Error: 406 Not Acceptable
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I’m facing an issue with my custom Managed RAP OData V4 service integrated with a Node.js CAPM-based custom Fiori app. The app is a read-only report running in the SAP BTP environment.

To address payload query length issues, we have enabled the POST operation, and token handling for the remote service is configured as follows:

Remote service CSRF-token handling 

To add POST support for this CDS-based root entity, I implemented a Behavior Definition to handle the create operation with Behavior Pool Class (with minimal code). 

Now service metadata shows the supported formats.

However, the payload, upon reaching the backend S/4HANA system, results in the following error:

 

 

 

Error: "The resource identified by the request is only capable of generating response entities which have content characteristics not acceptable according to the accept headers sent in the request."

 

 

 

Despite the metadata correctly showing application/json as a supported format, the error persists. Simplifying the scenario, a single GET request (outside of the batch request) with Accept: application/json works perfectly. However, the issue arises specifically with the batching mechanism for the POST operation.

I came across @Andre_Fischers blog on Function Import, which discusses this concept, but I am unclear on how to apply it within the RAP framework.

How can I address the issue with the batching mechanism for POST requests? Is there something specific in the RAP framework or the request construction that I might be overlooking?

Any insights, examples, or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support!

NW ABAP Gateway (OData) ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model Node.js SAP Fiori Elements  SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

entry.title: Replication flows failure notifications
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are exploring options to receive notifications whenever a replication flow encounters an error in any of its objects or the entire flow, similar to the notifications available for task chains.

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Re: OData service for Fiori Analytical Table - sap.ui.tab...
entry.content: Thank you very much Gregor, it is really helpful. Best regards!

entry.title: What is Nova Bot Access Code "NOVATO" and How to Use It
entry.content: The Nova Bot access code "NOVATO" gives you early access to exclusive features and tools. To use the code, simply enter it during registration to activate your account. Once activated, you'll be able to explore Nova Bot’s powerful tools, fast execution, and efficient performance. Don’t miss out—use the code now to experience everything Nova Bot has to offer!

entry.title: SAP ATTP. SSCC/SGTIN hierarchy as text and new select options. Idea and steps towards realization
entry.content:
Intro.

Transaction /STTP/Cockpit > Repository Browser > Serialized Containers > Object Hierarchy

The routine daily work of an SAP consultant often includes extracting hierarchies as text. This is necessary for customs declarations, manual EPCIS events, and many other purposes. For example, you might want to extract all SSCC boxes on an SSCC pallet or all SGTINs in an SSCC box (marked as 1 and 2 on the screenshot). However, this cannot be done directly in the transaction /STTP/Cockpit.

Missing but Essential Features

Another must-have feature that is currently missing is the ability to accept multiple inputs in the Transaction /STTP/Cockpit > Repository Browser > Lots, Serialized Trade Items, Serialized Containers, etc. (marked as 1 on the screenshot).

Proposed Solution

Idea

A custom report (and transaction) that supports the following inputs:

  • S_SSCC - SSCC (multiple input supported)

  • S_GTIN - GTIN (multiple input supported)

  • S_SGTIN - SGTIN (multiple input supported)

  • S_LOTNO - Lot number (multiple input supported)

  • S_DOCS - Document numbers (multiple input supported)

Input Options

  • P_EXACT - (Strictly SSCC boxes from the list. Behavior: If checked, the output includes information only about SSCCs in the input. If unchecked, information about all SSCC boxes on the SSCC pallet is included.)

  • P_EWM_F - (Behavior: If checked, the output is without symbols "()".)

  • P_SGTIN - (Behavior: If checked, the output hierarchy is SSCC pallet > SSCC box > SGTINs. If unchecked, the output hierarchy is SSCC pallet > SSCC box.)

  • P_NBRCT - (Behavior: If checked, the output is without segments: (00), (21). If unchecked, the output includes segments (00), (21).)

Output Options

  • P_CONT - Read hierarchy

  • P_STAT - Read statuses

  • P_DOCS - Read assigned documents

For all options, the output is an ALV report with Excel export functionality.

Output for Hierarchy

ALV List: SSCC Pallet, SSCC Box, GS1 GTIN, Lot Number, Qty SGTIN, Trade Item Description

Output for Assigned Statuses

ALV List: Object Code (EPC), Logistic Status, Pack Status, Disposition

Output for Assigned Documents

ALV List: SSCC Pallet, SSCC Box, Assigned Doc. Num

Export to a spreadsheet is available for all outputs (marked as 1 on the screenshot), and MS Excel (*.xlsx) format is also supported (marked as 2 on the screenshot).

Steps Toward Realization

  1. TYPES
    If you are not familiar with data types in the /STTP/ namespace, this collection of types will be a good starting point:
    TYPES.abap

  2. FORM HANDLER
    The START-OF-SELECTION event is raised by the ABAP runtime environment during the execution of an executable program after any standard selection screens have been processed:
     START-OF-SELECTION.abap

  3. DATA MINING
    The class /sttp/cl_dm_query is a central point for custom methods for ATTP data mining:
     ATTP_MISC_CLASS_METHODS

Conclusion

A useful custom report has been introduced, which can significantly aid the routine work of SAP ATTP consultants. If the described use case aligns with your system requirements, you can implement this report in your own system. The UI input/output options and steps for realization have been described in detail.

entry.title: Issues with creating Signup page
entry.content: Hello everyone, I am trying to create a Signup page for my app, I followed closely some videos I saw on youtube as well as do some reading around, just to fix it but I dont seem to see where the problem is. I have been stuck and I believe assistance from anyone at this point might help. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Sending mail from C4C when creating Email via OData
entry.content:
I know it's been a while, but in case someone stumbles across this, I had the same requirement. The issue is, that the Email is not send, because the action to send it is not executed in a create via oData. 

In order to make it work a small enhancement via SDK is possible. The send action is callable from the activity BO. 

So extend the activity BO and in the before save check if, it is a create, the user is the oData user and it is activity type. If the conditions are true the send action can be called.

BR

David

entry.title: What is the correct course for S/4 HANA public cloud project system or EPPM
entry.content:
What is the correct course for S/4 HANA public cloud project system or EPPM? I could only find one i.e. professional services. As per my understanding this is an industry specific learning.

Any inputs would be a great help.

entry.title: Re: Herencia de roles/derive roles
entry.content: Debes verificar que el rol padre este conforme, luego que generas los hijos actualizas el perfil del rol padre hacia todos los hijos hay una opción en la PFCG, cada vez que ejecutes alguna modificación la debes hacer en el padre.

entry.title: Re: I need to associate my previous certification with my...
entry.content:
SAP Support Portal – Try logging into the SAP Support Portal (support.sap.com) using any credentials you remember. If you have multiple S-User IDs, your account may be linked to more than one.

Contact SAP Support – If you still cannot retrieve it, contact SAP support by raising a request through the SAP Customer Interaction Center (CIC). They can help you recover your S-User ID based on your previous company details.

entry.title: Re: Post a 501 movement type using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content: Thank you @Harsh14 for your response.

entry.title: Re: How to download and install cloud connector in SAP BTP?
entry.content: You don't install cloud connector directly in BTP. Cloud Connector is to allow cloud applications in BTP to connect to SAP and/or other systems/applications inside a companies internal network.
